changelog.item: The custom filter for releases can now filter out irrelevant downloaded files at the same time.
Added sorting by the time a repository is starred.

Adjusted repository search: currently, entering a keyword triggers real-time repository name matching, while clicking AI Search will retrieve AI-analyzed content, tags, etc., speeding up search. Natural language search is temporarily disabled due to unsatisfactory results.
Add a check for updates feature
Persistent sorting for repository list
Added AI detection concurrency configuration
Unified repository card tag styles
Improved AI analysis failure indicators and added a retry button for failed items
fix: Fixed the issue where the analysis progress becomes 0 due to switching headers during AI analysis
Add drag and no-drag regions for desktop app
switch to infinite scroll with consistent card widths
fix: Sidebar now scrolls vertically when its content exceeds the screen height, preventing hidden or inaccessible items and improving navigation on smaller screens or long lists.
fix: Fixed webdav backup recovery issue.
feat: Supports multiple AI API formats and fixes connectivity testing.
feat: Added an optional Express + SQLite backend server, supporting cross-device automatic synchronization.
Added optional OpenAI Responses API support while preserving existing OpenAI Chat Completions compatibility.
Fixed repository update time display and sorting by prioritizing pushed_at, with fallback to updated_at.
Fixed white-screen crash when opening empty categories by correcting React Hook call order.
Fixed the desktop white-screen startup issue after IndexedDB migration by normalizing persisted state before first render.
Ensured seamless data migration from legacy localStorage to IndexedDB without requiring users to reset local data.
Kept rollback compatibility so users can return to older versions without losing existing data.
Added category lock support in repository edit panel to prevent sync from overwriting manual category choices.
Manual category changes now auto-enable lock by default, and users can still manually unlock later.
Improved edit modal defaults by preferring AI summary as editable custom description content.
Added repository unstar support and improved delete flow stability by handling 204 No Content responses and cleaning related release/subscription state.
Fixed desktop first-login token paste issue (Ctrl/Cmd + V) in v0.2.0-v0.2.2 builds.
Restored global search shortcuts and desktop Edit menu roles (cut/copy/paste/select all) for consistent keyboard behavior.
Improved sync stability, backend auth handling, and mobile layout refinements. (Thanks @HuberttFox for the contribution.)
Added a release list unsubscribe action with confirmation and immediate sync.
Fixed macOS App/Window menu missing in desktop build, restoring Command+Q (quit) and Command+W (close window) shortcuts.
Fixed SyntaxError in generated main.js caused by improper string escaping in the build workflow.
Kept already loaded repository cards visible during sync, so refreshes no longer interrupt reading flow.
Preserved scroll position after repository sync to avoid sudden jumps in long lists.
Persisted repository view context, including the selected category and current view, across refresh and reload.
Updated the app and login icon assets to use the project icon consistently.
Improved nginx proxy resilience for containerized deployments by using a more reliable backend upstream setup. Thanks to external contributor @HuberttFox for PR #60.
Added optional reasoning effort support for GPT-5 compatible OpenAI APIs.
Kept backward compatibility by leaving reasoning disabled unless users explicitly enable it.
Persisted the reasoning setting across frontend config state, backend config APIs, import/export, and database migration.
Improved compatibility for OpenAI Responses and reasoning-enabled requests with longer request and connection test timeouts.
Normalized legacy unsupported reasoning value `minimal` to `low` to prevent request failures with GPT-5 compatible models.
Added observable decryption failure handling for stored AI API keys, WebDAV passwords, and GitHub tokens.
Kept settings APIs responsive even when one stored secret cannot be decrypted, instead of failing the entire response.
Returned per-secret status metadata so the frontend can distinguish normal, empty, and decrypt-failed states.
Added clear Settings warnings telling users to re-enter and save configurations whose stored secrets can no longer be decrypted.
Improved debugging by logging decryption failures with config context on the backend.
Improved AI summary parsing by stripping fenced JSON code blocks before extraction.
Replaced fragile greedy JSON matching with balanced-brace parsing to better handle wrapped responses.
Increased the AI analysis token budget to reduce failures caused by truncated structured output.
Added hide/show for built-in categories - default categories can be hidden from sidebar and restored from Settings.
Added delete support for custom categories without affecting repository starred status.
Added drag-and-drop category assignment - drag repository cards onto sidebar categories to quickly assign.
Added category lock - manually assigned categories won't be changed by subsequent AI analysis or sync.
Added AI category matching - AI analysis now considers custom categories and assigns repositories accordingly.
Fixed Gemini connection test failures by increasing maxTokens threshold. Thanks to @Minttc for the contribution!
Fixed DeepSeek Reasoner compatibility by removing unsupported API parameters.
Fixed sidebar layout - category count badges are now properly aligned without layout shifts on hover.
Fixed backend persistence - category lock state is now properly synced to the database.
Fixed category rename - renaming a custom category now updates all assigned repositories.
Tabbed Settings Interface: Settings are now organized into dedicated panels (General, AI, WebDAV, Backup, Backend, Category, Data Management) with mobile-friendly tab navigation.
Repository Multi-Select and Bulk Actions: Select multiple repositories at once with a bulk actions toolbar, bulk categorization modal, and pipelined AI-summary bulk processing.
README Modal: View repository READMEs with an improved Markdown renderer featuring safer link and image handling.
Back-to-Top Button: Quick navigation for long lists.
WebDAV Backup/Restore: Backup your data to WebDAV and restore when needed.
Backend Sync Controls: Sync your data across devices with backend integration.
AI Config Management: Configure and manage AI service settings.
Preset Asset Filters: Quick filter presets for release assets.
Category Management and Ordering: Organize categories with drag-and-drop reordering.
Settings Refactor: Settings split into focused subpanels for better organization.
New UI utilities and animations (scrollbars, touch helpers, slide/shake/bounce effects).
README decoding now preserves UTF-8 encoding.
More robust Markdown and link handling.
Special thanks to @SummerRay160 for their tremendous contributions to this release!
AI Configuration Panel: Added connection testing for AI services to validate availability before use.
Prompt Management: Enhanced prompt editing and display logic with better management experience.
Default Category Override: Modify name, icon, and keywords for default categories with reset functionality.
Category Keywords: Added keyword support for categories with keyword input during creation/editing.
Trending View: New navigation entry with four channels - Most Stars, Most Forks, Top Developers, and Trending.
Batch AI Analysis: Reuse AIAnalysisOptimizer for subscription content with pause/resume/stop controls.
Backend Proxy: New Search API proxy routes for backend mode supporting GitHub Search.
Trending Filters: Time-range (daily/weekly/monthly) and language input for trending discovery.
Improved responsive layout for better mobile and desktop adaptation.
Full Chinese and English localization for all trending channels.
Fixed merge conflicts and tag rendering issues in subscription feature.
Special thanks to @SummerRay160 (PR #84, #85) and @chan-yuu (PR #86) for their contributions!
Discovery View Refactor: Replaced subscription view with a new Discovery view featuring channel-based browsing, multi-filter sorting, search, pagination, and a "Load more" button. (PR #90)
Enhanced Markdown renderer with syntax highlighting, copyable code blocks, image zoom/modal, in-modal TOC, heading navigation, and adjustable font size. (PR #90)
Added in-page AI analysis controls for repository cards with star/unstar support and safer clipboard helpers. (PR #90)
Improved responsive layout with Inter font family, refined breakpoints, and lucide-react icons throughout. (PR #90)
Switched Trending data source from GitHub Search API to GitHubTrendingRSS for more reliable results, with daily/weekly/monthly time-range filter. (PR #96)
Added RSS description parsing with HTML entity decoding, and supplemented RSS data with GitHub API for missing repository fields. (PR #96)
Fixed macOS desktop white-screen crash by removing Inter/CDN stylesheet loading and hardening discovery state rehydration. (PR #95)
Removed auto-loading pagination from Discovery view and fixed null/empty description handling in RepositoryEditModal. (PR #97)
Special thanks to @SummerRay160 for their outstanding contributions to this release (PR #90, #97)!
